supposed to be upper $20s. (Condon/Moens). 
Real estate recovery: A comeback story for houses on 
Palm Beach 
By Darrell Hofheinz 
Daily News Real Estate Writer 
Updated: 6:23 p.m. Saturday, May 21, 2011 
Posted: 5:53 p.m. Saturday, May 21, 2011 
At this time last year, plenty of Palm Beach real estate agents were still trying to forget 2009, a year when lackluster home 
sales and plummeting home prices proved the island wasn't nearly as insulated from the nation's property woes as many had 
thought. 
But this season, those same agents have watched as things finally began to make their way back to normal in the single-
family home market — or at least to some semblance to normal. The past six months, especially, have seen

Thanks for your help. I don't know how to say that strongly enough. 
I think we are on a good track now. 
I did check that I paid a gift tax on it. I can't send you the legal opinion for a couple of reasons but the question is simply 
whether it is a gift or for services rendered. 
If someone has been doing the same services for the same salary which is market endorsed salary using outside HR advice 
and you decide to make a gift which you pay the gift tax on the presumption is in your favor. 
There can't be any services rendered related to it or then it is taxable to the recipient. I will bet I am more careful about 
gift taxes than almost any other tax payer.
